own_review_points: Read path multiplies HMS seconds by MICROSECONDS_PER_SECOND without multiplyExact (no overflow in practice for valid epochs)
Write uses floorDiv(min)/ceilDiv(max) so round-trip widens the [min,max] range -> conservative/correct for stats estimates
getTimestampEpochMicro truncates nanos->micros even for max (documented as acceptable for estimates)
Splitting TimestampType from TimestampWithTimeZoneType in getSupportedColumnStatistics is the correct boundary
Documented TODO (issue 28835): columnTypes resolves TIMESTAMP to millis ignoring session precision -- pre-existing, author-flagged
notes: Correct, low-severity PR; human ref's only point is a confirmation (product tests verify truncation), not a bug. V8 P1 (multiplyExact consistency) and P2 (floorDiv/ceilDiv vs plain truncation asymmetry note) are substantive minor observations = good. P3,P4,P5,P6 are correct but praise/restating = trivial. No wrongs, no fabs; all cited content within visible diff. The human's confirmation point is implicitly covered by V8's nanoseconds/floor-ceil verification, so no genuine miss. Modest positive.
